key_insight: A hybrid sharp-diffuse interface method separates heat transfer and flow modeling to accurately capture rapid evaporation and temperature-driven forces in laser-metal melt pools.
engineering_relevance: It improves prediction fidelity for laser beam welding and laser powder bed fusion by resolving critical interface temperatures that strongly affect recoil pressure, surface tension, and melt pool dynamics.
method: Sharp-interface CutFEM for the thermal problem to resolve steep temperature gradients accurately.
Diffuse-interface level-set one-fluid FEM for multiphase flow with large density ratios and moving interfaces.
Couples both parts by extending the sharp temperature field into a narrow interface region for force evaluation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports second-order spatial convergence for the sharp thermal model, two orders of magnitude larger mesh sizes for 1 accuracy versus diffuse methods, and about 10x better accuracy than a purely diffuse model on the same mesh in a coupled benchmark.
watch_item: The approach adds coupling complexity and requires careful consistent transfer of sharp-interface temperature into the diffuse flow formulation.
paper_title: A hybrid sharp-diffuse interface approach to accurately model melt pool dynamics with rapid evaporation in laser-based processing of metals
